newsSince: No material company-specific news since June 9. AZZ's last report was FY2026 full-year results on April 22 (sales $1.65B +4.6%, Metal Coatings $758.7M +14.1%, FY27 guide reiterated up to $1.775B sales / $400M EBITDA) — already known at the June 9 case. Stock rose +8.25% since June 9 on no fresh fundamental data.

verdicts.rankRationale: No new evidence since June 9; FY26 results (Apr 22) and FY27 guide were already known. Second-derivative electrification/grid-protection thesis (galvanizing for substations, DC steel) unchanged. The +8.25% pop is price, not a thesis shift; hold the latent-node rank.
verdicts.bull: Galvanizing demand from grid/transmission and AI-datacenter steel; Metal Coatings +14%.
verdicts.neutral: Solid second-derivative electrification play, but indirect AI link and modest growth.
verdicts.bear: Cyclical construction exposure; rerated +42% YTD on no fresh catalyst.

verdicts.rankRationale: June 9 already nudged it down at the top of the strict screen for rerating-without-evidence. Since then still no new fundamental data; the +8.25% drift continues the price-only run. On the strict residual-asymmetry lens the latent claim is unchanged, not freshly eroded, so hold at rank 4.
verdicts.bull: Hot-dip galvanizing/coil coating protects steel as compute becomes more physical infrastructure.
verdicts.neutral: Quality electrification proxy, but AI link is indirect and well-flagged.
verdicts.bear: At a top-4 strict slot, +42% YTD rerating with no new evidence dilutes the 'latent' premium.
